info.title: WebMS Intra REST API
info.description: Internal portal platform REST API. Two mutually-exclusive auth modes are accepted on every endpoint: (1) an authenticated portal session (cookie-based) plus a valid CSRF token on write operations (passed via `X-CSRF-TOKEN` header or `csrf_token` body field), or (2) a site-scoped bearer API key (`Authorization: Bearer wbms_…`, minted at Admin → Integrations → API Keys). Bearer requests need NO CSRF token — CSRF protects cookie-based sessions from cross-site forgery and is meaningless for a bearer token that travels in an explicit Authorization header set by the calling code, never by a browser automatically. Bearer requests are tenant-pinned to the key's own site (regardless of the Host header) and are rate-limited per key (see `/api/v1/*` paths below). Every endpoint can be enabled/disabled per-site via the `api.{module}.{action}.enabled` setting; bearer requests resolve that flag against the KEY's site, not the host-detected default. The `/api/v1/{resource}[/{id}]` paths are a thin RESTful facade: each verb maps onto the SAME handler file and the SAME `api.{app}.{action}.enabled` flag as its documented `/api/{app}/{action}` legacy alias — there are no separate permissions to manage.

servers.description: Same-origin (whichever portal is serving this page)

tags.name: Noticeboard
tags.description: Visual poster wall (Canva embeds, image/video/text posters)

tags.name: Expenses
tags.description: Expense claims, line items, and (session-only) approval workflow.

tags.name: Legacy Alias
tags.description: Pre-v1 endpoint path (`/api/{app}/{action}`). Still fully supported and documented — the /api/v1/{resource} path for the same operation is a thin facade over the identical handler file and enablement flag.

sessionCookie.name: PHPSESSID
sessionCookie.description: Portal session cookie set on sign-in.

csrfToken.name: X-CSRF-TOKEN
csrfToken.description: CSRF token. Read from `<meta name="csrf-token">` in the portal HTML or from the session. Required on POST endpoints; ignored on read-only GETs.

bearerAuth.description: Site-scoped API key from Admin → Integrations → API Keys; scopes gate each resource:verb; rate-limited per key; CSRF-exempt.

NoticeboardPoster.description: Poster as returned by GET /api/noticeboard/list (DB columns already mapped to the field names the board component reads).

id.description: Client-facing id; the literal string "p" + posterID.

kicker.description: Short eyebrow line shown above the title.

date.description: Set when schedule=once; empty string otherwise.

time.description: 24-hour HH:MM, or empty string if not set.

link.description: Official event page. The board opens this when the enlarged poster is tapped a second time.

mediaType.description: Text-only posters (no image/canva) are reported here as "image" with an empty image URL.

canva.description: Canva embed URL, for mediaType canva.

thumb.description: Optional board thumbnail override for Canva posters.

colorIndex.description: Index into the board's built-in accent-colour palette.

serif.description: true = editorial serif title font, false = bold sans-serif.

PosterInput.description: One poster in the `posters` array sent to POST /api/noticeboard/save. The client always sends the FULL desired board state — any existing poster whose id is missing from the array is soft-deleted.

id.description: Pass the existing "p<posterID>" to update that poster. Omit, or use a fresh client-generated id (e.g. "p<timestamp>"), to insert a new one — an id that doesn't belong to this site is treated as new rather than overwriting another site's poster.

title.description: Required — rows with an empty/whitespace-only title are silently skipped.

link.description: http(s):// absolute or a root-relative ("/…") path only — any other scheme (e.g. javascript:) is silently stripped to empty.

mediaType.description: Server-side derived value wins: forced to "canva" if canva is set, or "text" if neither canva nor image is set.

image.description: Media URL for image/video posters. data: URIs are rejected with 422 — upload via the media library and pass its URL instead.

canva.description: Canva embed/share URL. Must start with https://www.canva.com/ or it is discarded.

V1SuccessEnvelope.description: Standard success envelope returned by every /api/v1/* and legacy /api/{app}/{action} endpoint.

data.description: Endpoint-specific payload — see each operation's response schema.

detail.description: Only present when the server is running in debug mode.

serviceTypeID.description: FK tblAttendanceServiceTypes; must exist for the current site.

eventID.description: FK tblEvents; must exist for the current site.

AttendanceUpdate.description: PATCH-style — only fields present are touched. When `counts` is present, ALL existing headcount rows for the session are replaced (delete + re-insert).

DocumentCreate.description: Accepts EITHER multipart/form-data (file field `file`, session-UI parity) OR application/json with base64 `fileContent` (shown here — the bearer/device mode). The real MIME type is sniffed from the file bytes (finfo) and checked against a server-side allowlist — the client-declared type/extension is never trusted.

categoryID.description: FK tblDocCategories; must exist for the current site.

fileName.description: Original filename — cosmetic only; the stored filename + extension are server-generated from the sniffed MIME type.

fileContent.description: Base64-encoded file bytes. Capped by the `documents.api.maxUploadBytes` setting (default 10485760).

DocumentUpdate.description: Metadata only — v1 has no file-replacement endpoint (rotate via create + delete).

ExpenseCreate.description: `lineTotal` and the claim `totalAmount` are always computed server-side from `unitCost * quantity` — never accepted from the client. New claims always land in 'Pending' status; the approval/status-transition workflow is DEFERRED to Phase 3 (no `PUT /api/v1/expenses/{id}` exists in v1).

deptID.description: FK tblDepts; must exist for the current site.

userID.description: REQUIRED for bearer (API-key) submissions only — the owning user, who must belong to the key's own site. Ignored/ not required in session mode, where the owner is always the authenticated session user.

UserCreate.description: Passwords are NEVER accepted here — the new account gets a random, unusable password hash and must go through the existing forgot-password flow to set a real one. `isAdmin` (portal-wide) is honoured in session mode only — a bearer key can never mint a portal admin, even with `users:write`; use `isSiteAdmin` (site-scoped) instead.

isAdmin.description: Portal-wide admin flag. Session mode only — silently ignored for bearer callers.

UserUpdate.description: Editable: fullName, isActive, isAdmin (session mode only), isSiteAdmin. Email address and password are excluded from v1 (silently ignored if present). A session caller can never deactivate their OWN account via this endpoint.

isAdmin.description: Portal-wide admin flag. Session mode only — silently ignored for bearer callers.

401.description: Auth required

403.description: Endpoint disabled

get.summary: Fetch a single event by ID or slug

404.description: Not found

400.description: Validation error

400.description: Validation error

get.summary: List attendance sessions for the current site
List active announcements for the current site
List active posters on the current site's noticeboard
get.description: Any authenticated user may read. Returns every non-deleted poster for the current site, ordered by manual sort order then event date.

401.description: Auth required

403.description: Endpoint disabled

get.summary: Render a QR code image for a noticeboard share link
get.description: Any authenticated user may read. `data` must be this site's own host if it includes a scheme — the endpoint refuses to act as an open QR redirector for arbitrary third-party URLs.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Text or URL to encode. Must resolve to this same host when it contains a scheme (e.g. https://…).

200.description: QR image — PNG when the gd extension is available, SVG fallback otherwise (Content-Type reflects the actual format returned).

401.description: Auth required

403.description: Endpoint disabled

422.description: Missing/oversized data parameter, or data does not resolve to this host

post.summary: Replace the current site's poster set (site admins only)
post.description: Site admins only (App::isSiteAdmin()) — CSRF-protected via the X-CSRF-TOKEN header. The client sends the FULL desired poster array in one call; posters upsert by their "p<posterID>" id (a foreign or new-looking id inserts a fresh row instead of overwriting another tenant's poster), and any existing poster left out of the array is soft-deleted. The whole batch is validated before any row is written, and rolled back together on failure.

400.description: Invalid CSRF token

403.description: Admin access required, or endpoint disabled

422.description: Malformed payload — missing `posters` array, or a poster's image is a data: URI (upload via the media library instead)

500.description: Save failed — the whole batch was rolled back

get.summary: List leadership roles and current assignees
List tasks (own by default; admins can filter via userID)
List prayer requests visible to the current user
get.description: Non-admins see their own requests plus congregation-visible ones. Admins see all. Anonymous-flagged requests have `submitterID` nulled for non-admin readers.

get.summary: List documents for the current site
List users (admin only)

403.description: Admin required

get.summary: Healthcheck (no auth required)
get.description: Uptime probe with DB ping. Returns 200/`ok` on full health, 503/`degraded` when the DB is unreachable.

get.summary: List published events for the current site (v1)
get.description: Requires scope `events:read` (bearer) or an authenticated session. **Legacy alias:** `GET /api/events/list`.

401.description: Authentication required — no valid session and no valid bearer API key.

403.description: Forbidden — bearer key missing the required scope, session caller failing the role gate, or the endpoint disabled via the api.{app}.{action}.enabled setting (same flag as the legacy alias).

405.description: Method not allowed for this resource/shape — see the `Allow` response header for the verbs this resource actually supports.

Allow.description: Comma-separated list of supported HTTP verbs.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded (bearer only) — see the `Retry-After` response header.

Retry-After.description: Seconds until the oldest in-window hit ages out.

404.description: Resource not found — including a cross-site id probe, which 404s rather than 403 so it never confirms another tenant’s row exists.

405.description: Method not allowed for this resource/shape — see the `Allow` response header for the verbs this resource actually supports.

Allow.description: Comma-separated list of supported HTTP verbs.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded (bearer only) — see the `Retry-After` response header.

Retry-After.description: Seconds until the oldest in-window hit ages out.

get.summary: List documents for the current site (v1)
get.description: Requires scope `documents:read` (bearer) or an authenticated session. **Legacy alias:** `GET /api/documents/list`.

401.description: Authentication required — no valid session and no valid bearer API key.

post.summary: Upload a document to the library (v1, #157 remnant)
post.description: Requires scope `documents:write` (bearer) or an admin session. Accepts either multipart/form-data (file field `file`) or the JSON base64 `fileContent` shape documented on `DocumentCreate` — see that schema for the finfo MIME-sniffing and size-cap rules. **Legacy alias:** `POST /api/documents/create`.

get.summary: List expense claims for the current site (v1)
get.description: Requires scope `expenses:read` (bearer) or an authenticated session. **Legacy alias:** `GET /api/expenses/list`.

401.description: Authentication required — no valid session and no valid bearer API key.

403.description: Forbidden — bearer key missing the required scope, session caller failing the role gate, or the endpoint disabled via the api.{app}.{action}.enabled setting (same flag as the legacy alias).

post.description: Requires scope `expenses:write` (bearer) or any authenticated session (submitting is a member action, not admin-only). `lineTotal`/`totalAmount` are always computed server-side. Bearer callers MUST supply `userID` in the body (the owning user, checked against the key's own site) since a bearer request carries no session owner. **Status-transition update (approve/reject/reimburse) is DEFERRED to Phase 3** — there is no `PUT /api/v1/expenses/{id}` in this release. **Legacy alias:** `POST /api/expenses/create`.

400.description: Validation error — missing/invalid field, or (on an /{id} path) a non-numeric resource id.

Allow.description: Comma-separated list of supported HTTP verbs.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded (bearer only) — see the `Retry-After` response header.

Retry-After.description: Seconds until the oldest in-window hit ages out.

delete.summary: Delete an expense claim (v1; only status = Pending may be deleted)
delete.description: Requires scope `expenses:write` (bearer) or an owner-or-admin session. Bearer mode: scope + the key's site-pin is sufficient (no separate ownership check — a key already operates within one site). Returns 409 if the claim has left the 'Pending' status. **Legacy alias:** `POST /api/expenses/delete?id={id}`.

401.description: Authentication required — no valid session and no valid bearer API key.

get.summary: List active posters on the current site’s noticeboard (v1)
get.description: Requires scope `noticeboard:read` (bearer) or an authenticated session. **Legacy alias:** `GET /api/noticeboard/list`.

401.description: Authentication required — no valid session and no valid bearer API key.

post.summary: Replace the current site’s poster set (v1; `POST` aliases the `save` action)
post.description: Requires scope `noticeboard:write` (bearer) or a site-admin session. There is no per-poster PUT/PATCH/DELETE in v1 — the client always sends the FULL desired board state in one call; posters upsert by their `"p<posterID>"` id, and any existing poster left out of the array is soft-deleted. See `PosterInput`. **Legacy alias:** `POST /api/noticeboard/save`.

post.summary: Create a user (v1, deferral item 3 — highest blast-radius surface)
post.description: Admin ALWAYS required in both modes — bearer additionally needs `users:write` **and** the `api.users.create.enabled` flag, seeded `'false'` (default-off; an admin must consciously enable it). Passwords are never accepted — see `UserCreate`. There is no DELETE for this resource in v1. **Legacy alias:** none — this endpoint is new in v1, exposed only at this path.
